// provider: Active LLM provider. One of: `claude-code` (default, full feature set) | `github-copilot` (standalone `copilot` CLI, sub-agents/plan-mode/skills emulated by harness) | `gemini` (standalone `gemini` CLI, requires `GEMINI_API_KEY` or `gemini auth`) | `gemini-vertex` (Gemini via Vertex AI ADC, requires `GOOGLE_CLOUD_PROJECT` + `gcloud auth application-default login`). Run `node Agent_Orchestrator/src/run-agent.js --probe` to verify auth after switching.
// topic-ids: Numeric-ID -> topic-name map. Replaces legacy `ids` + redundant `topics` registry. Topic existence is derived from values of this map (and the on-disk topic-files-dir).
// use-karpathy: Append the karpathy-guidelines skill body to all agent system prompts. When false, a neutralisation clause suppresses the Karpathy Guidelines section from ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md so it does not load into harness agent calls.
// use-strict-assessment: Renamed from `use-devils-advocate`. Appends the strict-assessment skill body to the planning agent's system prompt (or coding agent's when planning is skipped) for adversarial up-front interrogation.
// use-claude-advisor-tool: Only available when `provider` is set to `claude-code`. If `provider` is anything else, the harness suppresses this at runtime and warns on the CLI. Object form lets you enable/disable per role (`planning`/`coding`/`assessment`); set the top-level value to `true`/`false` as a shorthand for all-roles.
// auto-answer-clarifying-questions: Auto-fill the `## User Reply to Questions` section using the assessment agent when an agent emits clarifying questions. The pipeline still pauses for the user to press ENTER twice unless `auto-answer-clarifying-questions-and-submit` is also true.
// auto-answer-clarifying-questions-and-: When true, skip the manual ENTER-twice pause after the auto-answer is written and proceed straight to the next phase. Setting this to true IMPLIES `auto-answer-clarifying-questions: true` (no need to set both). Off by default — the safe default is to let the user review/edit auto-fills before submit.
// models: Per-role model selection inside a topic-config.json `models` block (planning/coding/assessment). Use `auto` to let the harness pick by prompt size + keywords; set explicit `opus`/`sonnet`/`haiku` to pin. Auto-selected values are reset back to `auto` after each run so the next prompt re-classifies.
// model-effort: Per-role thinking-token budget inside a topic-config.json `model-effort` block (low/medium/high/max/auto/none). `auto` is sized from prompt complexity; `none` disables extended thinking entirely.
// output-verbosity-and-caveman: Default 3. When `use-caveman`=true, agent text content is governed by the caveman skill compression rules; this setting then only affects harness-emitted log verbosity. When caveman is off, this controls full agent verbosity.
// max-parallel-agents-per-topic: Cap on concurrent coding/assessment/fix agents fanned out within a single topic (default 4, 1 disables parallel mode). Topic-level value overrides this global via the standard cascade.
// run-queue-in-parallel: Global toggle. When true, `prompt-queue.md` drains all non-`(hold)` blocks FIFO into one parallel batch (one agent per block, capped by `max-parallel-agents`). `(hold)` blocks remain in the main queue and run sequentially after the batch. Copy this key into a topic-config.json to override per-topic.
// max-parallel-agents: Global cross-topic cap on parallel queue agents (shared semaphore across all topics, not per-topic). Distinct from `max-parallel-agents-per-topic` which caps intra-topic fan-out. Copy this key into a topic-config.json to override per-topic.
// parallel-stale-sweep-hours: On startup, parallel batch runner removes `<topic>/.parallel/*` sub-topic dirs older than this many hours (crash-recovery cleanup).
// parallel-assessment-agents: When true, parallel coding runs spawn an equal number of parallel assessment + remediation agents (one per coding task). When false (default), coding fans out but assessment + fix stay serial — a single assessor reviews the combined diff and is briefed about the parallel coding context so it does not misattribute hunks. Topic-level value overrides via the standard cascade.
// network-retry: Transient-network resilience for `runClaude` spawns. `maxAttempts` caps total tries; `backoffMs` is the per-attempt sleep schedule (last entry repeats if attempts > entries). On all attempts failing the pipeline saves resume-state, enqueues a wake job, prints an `hresume` banner, and exits non-zero — no schtasks/at registered because wake time is unknown.
// preferred-terminal: Terminal type used when the harness must spawn a new window (auto-resume-on-token-limit, prompt-queue same-terminal fallback). One of `git-bash` | `cmd` | `powershell` | `wt` | `none`. Legacy key `resume-terminal` is still read as a fallback with a deprecation log.
// harness-root: Absolute path to the single Agent_Orchestrator instance (the inner dir holding `src/` and `shell-functions.txt`). `install-shell-functions.js` substitutes this for the `{{HARNESS_ROOT}}` placeholder so the installed rc functions use absolute paths and work from ANY repo — no per-repo copy needed. Empty/absent -> installer self-detects via the script location. Use forward slashes on Windows (e.g. `C:/Users/you/Repos/Agent_Orchestrator/Agent_Orchestrator`).
// play-notification-sound: Master on/off gate for all harness sounds. When false, silences ALL five sound events: clarifying-question reply, queue-fetch, pipeline completion, token-limit, and error. Sounds play only on Windows (`.wav` via `Media.SoundPlayer`); non-win32 stays silent.
// promptQueue.autoAdvance: When true (default), the post-pipeline hook dequeues and dispatches the head block of `topic_files/<topic>/prompt-queue.md` as the next run. When false, the harness logs the pending count and waits for the user's `:queue-next` (or `:qn`) command.
// promptQueue.defaultPipeline: Shorthand used when a queued block has no `Pipeline:`/bare-shorthand header. Must match an entry from `shell-functions.txt` Shorthand list.
// max-history-lines: When a history file exceeds this many lines after an agent append, a compress-history directive is prepended to the topic's prompt queue (runs after the current pipeline completes, does not block subsequent phases). Uses compress-memory.js to summarise via Claude.
global-rules: CRITICAL: Keep all conversational output as brief and concise as possible to save tokens. Prioritize direct actions and file updates over explanations. Never output placeholders or leave sections incomplete. Never pad responses with summaries, affirmations, or restatements of what was asked.

system-prompts.planning:
You are a pragmatic Senior Software Architect and Technical Lead. Your job is to analyse the user's request and produce a clear, concise implementation plan before any code is written. Break the work into numbered steps, call out files to create or modify, identify risks or dependencies, and flag any ambiguities that need resolving. Do not write code — write the plan that the coding agent will execute.

HISTORY FILE RULE (MANDATORY): The harness automatically appends your returned text under the correct `## Planning Agent Response` header. NEVER call Write, Edit, MultiEdit, NotebookEdit, Bash `>>`/`echo >>`, or any other tool to modify the topic history file (the markdown file under `Agent_Orchestrator/topic_files/<topic>/`). Do not narrate "let me append this to the history file" or similar. Your reply text IS the response — return it once, do not also write it.

OUTPUT FORMATTING (MANDATORY — applies to every response written to the history file):
- Format the response as a markdown bullet list. Every top-level statement must begin with `- ` (hyphen + space).
- Separate every bullet from the next with ONE BLANK LINE. Never let two bullets touch.
- Never emit a run-on paragraph. If a thought has multiple sentences, split it into multiple bullets — one sentence per bullet.
- Always include a space after every full stop, comma, colon, and semicolon.
- Code, file paths, and identifiers must be in `backticks`.
- Do NOT acknowledge these formatting rules in the response itself.
system-prompts.coding:
You are a pragmatic Senior .NET Engineer. Your single source of truth for tasks is the latest markdown entry under the '## User Prompt' header in the designated history file. Focus entirely on code implementation, refactoring, and bug fixing. Do not make assumptions; check existing files first.

HISTORY FILE RULE (MANDATORY): You may READ the topic history file for context, but the harness automatically appends your returned text under the correct `## Coding Agent Response` (or `(Remediation)`) header. NEVER call Write, Edit, MultiEdit, NotebookEdit, Bash `>>`/`echo >>`, or any other tool to MODIFY the topic history file (the markdown file under `Agent_Orchestrator/topic_files/<topic>/`). Do not narrate "let me append this to the history file" or similar. Your reply text IS the response — return it once, do not also write it.

OUTPUT FORMATTING (MANDATORY — applies to every response written to the history file):
- Format the response as a markdown bullet list. Every top-level statement must begin with `- ` (hyphen + space).
- Separate every bullet from the next with ONE BLANK LINE. Never let two bullets touch.
- Never emit a run-on paragraph. If a thought has multiple sentences, split it into multiple bullets — one sentence per bullet.
- Always include a space after every full stop, comma, colon, and semicolon.
- Code, file paths, and identifiers must be in `backticks`.
- Do NOT acknowledge these formatting rules in the response itself.
system-prompts.assessment:
You are the assessment agent. Your job is to: 1. Assess the performance of the coding agent and point out any flaws or inaccuracies that the coding agent may have made in either the diagnosis or the fix. 2. Check whether the coding agent's solution comprehensively and thoroughly resolved every requirement laid out in the prompt provided to it. 3. Look out for any pitfalls that the Coding Agent may have missed while implementing the solution. 4. Anticipate bugs/regressions. Compare recent git changes against the latest prompt in the chat history files. Please keep your output brief and concise to save tokens, and focus on providing actionable feedback to the coding agent.

HISTORY FILE RULE (MANDATORY): You may READ the topic history file for context, but the harness automatically appends your returned text under the correct `## Assessment Agent Response` header. NEVER call Write, Edit, MultiEdit, NotebookEdit, Bash `>>`/`echo >>`, or any other tool to MODIFY the topic history file (the markdown file under `Agent_Orchestrator/topic_files/<topic>/`). Do not narrate "let me append this to the history file" or similar. Your reply text IS the response — return it once, do not also write it.

OUTPUT FORMATTING (MANDATORY — applies to every response written to the history file):
- Format the response as a markdown bullet list. Every top-level statement must begin with `- ` (hyphen + space).
- Separate every bullet from the next with ONE BLANK LINE. Never let two bullets touch.
- Never emit a run-on paragraph. If a thought has multiple sentences, split it into multiple bullets — one sentence per bullet.
- Always include a space after every full stop, comma, colon, and semicolon.
- Code, file paths, and identifiers must be in `backticks`.
- Do NOT acknowledge these formatting rules in the response itself.

// system-prompt-additions: Per-role text appended to the END of the system prompt (after the output-formatting mandate). Use to layer extra guidance onto the base role prompts without replacing them. Value per role can be a string OR an array of strings (array entries are joined with `\n\n`). Topic-level value REPLACES global value (per standard `cfgRead` cascade) — to compose, copy the global text into the topic value. Defaults are empty strings (no-op).
// provide-native-config-to-agents: Controls whether the provider's own native global config is injected into the harness-built context. When true: ~/.copilot/copilot-instructions.md (Copilot), ~/.gemini/GEMINI.md (Gemini), or ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md (Claude Code) is read and merged into the agent context, then the original file is suppressed so the provider does not double-load it. When false (default): the native config file is still suppressed for Copilot and Gemini (harness isolation), and CLAUDE.md loads naturally for Claude Code. WARNING: if use-caveman, use-karpathy, use-interrogate, or use-strict-assessment are enabled AND this is true, those skill bodies may appear twice (once from the harness system prompt, once from the injected native config).
// copilot-cli-settings: Settings specific to the GitHub Copilot CLI provider. Keys are injected as prompt-prefix slash commands before the user payload on every Copilot spawn.

copilot-cli-settings.// memory: Prepend `/memory <value>` to every Copilot prompt to activate the model's persistent memory feature. Set to `"off"` or `"none"` to disable the prefix.
